page: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The configuration includes 64 virtual CPUs (32 physical cores with hyper-threading) and 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with 2458 GB of local disk capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. There are no GPUs included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with the stress-ng multi-core test and Geekbench multi-core score placing in the top 25%. Memory performance is also robust, showing strong large-block read and write bandwidth, alongside a strong PassMark memory score. The processor's cryptographic and vector extensions, including AVX-512 and AES, contribute to strong performance in encryption, physics simulations, and software compilation. However, single-core performance benchmarks, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core, fall into the average tier.

This server is highly suited for database operations, caching layers, and web serving, as evidenced by strong Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput. It is also well-suited for parallelized tasks like software compilation and image processing. While it handles prompt processing for small and medium language models adequately, it exhibits weak performance in text generation for large 70B parameter models due to the lack of hardware GPU acceleration. The qualitative cost profile is balanced, offering high resource density across compute, memory, and local storage without the premium cost of specialized GPU hardware.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DDSv5 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 64 vCPUs, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, with Premium Storage capability. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, as validated by top-tier benchmark results in software compilation, database operations, and Redis caching. Single-core performance and large-scale LLM text generation represent relative performance tradeoffs. This server is highly suited for database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, static web serving, and software development pipelines.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ds_v5 features 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2.4 TB local storage, optimized for databases and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density general-purpose virtual machine featuring sixty-four virtual cores, substantial local storage, and strong multi-threaded performance.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 64 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM
Bundled 2458 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
Optimized for database hosting, Redis caching, and web serving
